title: Continuing Bonds Reflection
description: This reflection helps you explore and nurture your ongoing connection with who or what you've lost. It's not about 'moving on' — it's about discovering how this bond can continue to be part of your life in meaningful ways.
instructions: Choose a memory, quality, or aspect of your loss that feels important to you right now. Work through the prompts gently — there's no rush. You can return to this reflection many times with different memories.

fields.label: 1. Who or what are you remembering right now?
fields.placeholder: e.g., My mother, my partner, a friendship that ended, my health before the diagnosis, a career I loved

fields.label: 2. A specific memory, quality, or value that comes to mind
fields.placeholder: e.g., The way she laughed at her own jokes. His patience when I was struggling. The feeling of belonging I had in that role. Their unconditional kindness.

fields.label: 3. How does sitting with this memory feel right now? (0 = very painful, 100 = very comforting)
4. What type of continuing bond feels most meaningful to you?

options.label: Memory Keeping — photos, stories, shared memories
Values & Legacy — carrying forward what they taught or stood for
Ritual & Tradition — candles, visits, traditions you shared
Meaningful Object — something tangible that connects you
Internal Conversation — talking to them in your mind or prayers
Action & Service — honoring them through volunteering, creating, or giving

fields.label: 5. What did this person or experience teach you or give you?
fields.placeholder: e.g., She taught me that it's okay to ask for help. He showed me what patience looks like. That job taught me I was more capable than I thought.

fields.label: 6. How might you carry this forward? (A ritual, an activity, a value, a conversation)
fields.placeholder: e.g., I could cook their recipe on their birthday each year. I want to practice the patience they modeled. I could write a letter telling them what they meant to me.

fields.label: 7. A message to yourself about this connection
fields.placeholder: e.g., This bond doesn't end because they're gone. I carry them with me, and that's not holding on too tight — it's love continuing in a different form.

fields.label: 8. How do you feel now? (0 = very painful, 100 = very comforting)

distortionsList.name: Memory Keeping
distortionsList.description: Preserving and sharing memories through photos, videos, stories, and conversations. Keeping the person or experience alive through remembrance.

distortionsList.name: Values & Legacy
distortionsList.description: Carrying forward what the person taught you, stood for, or modeled. Living out values that honor their influence on your life.

distortionsList.name: Ritual & Tradition
distortionsList.description: Continuing shared traditions, creating new rituals (lighting candles, visiting meaningful places), or marking meaningful dates in ways that honor the bond.

distortionsList.name: Meaningful Object
distortionsList.description: Keeping or wearing something tangible — jewelry, clothing, a favorite mug, a handwritten note — that maintains a physical connection to what was lost.

distortionsList.name: Internal Conversation
distortionsList.description: Talking to the person in your mind, in prayer, or out loud. Asking what they would think, sharing your day, or simply saying 'I miss you.'

distortionsList.name: Action & Service
distortionsList.description: Honoring the person through volunteering, donating, advocating, creating (art, music, writing), or acts of kindness in their name or memory.

disclaimer: This reflection is for personal exploration and is not therapy. If your grief feels overwhelming or you are struggling to function, please reach out to a grief-informed therapist or counselor. If you are having thoughts of ending your life, call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) immediately.
